title: Bitter Dense Venezuelan Onion Stew
description: A hearty and bitter stew originating from Venezuela, featuring caramelized onions and a Mediterranean twist, perfect for late-night cravings. This dense stew is sure to become a favorite.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-low heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes or until they are deep golden brown and caramelized.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the anchovy paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pitted kalamata olives, chopped artichoke hearts, red wine vinegar, chicken broth, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley or oregano, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
